standard.notes: 'Bureau' has two meanings: the room ('le bureau' = the office) and the piece of furniture ('un bureau' = a desk). 'Au bureau' = at the office. 'Travailler dans un bureau' = to work in an office. 'Le bureau de poste' = the post office. Context makes the meaning clear.
examples: Je travaille au bureau tous les jours.
I work at the office every day.
Mon bureau est au troisième étage.
My office is on the third floor.

standard.notes: Feminine form: 'la collègue'. 'Mes collègues' = my colleagues. French workplace culture values formal politeness — using 'vous' with colleagues is still common in many sectors, especially at first. 'Un bon collègue' = a good colleague / a good mate at work.
examples: Je déjeune avec mes collègues.
I have lunch with my colleagues.
Ma collègue s'appelle Sophie.
My (female) colleague is called Sophie.

standard.notes: The French word 'ordinateur' was coined in 1955 by linguist Jacques Perret for IBM France — it comes from Latin 'ordinator' (one who orders). Informal: 'l'ordi'. 'L'ordinateur portable' = the laptop. 'Allumer / éteindre l'ordinateur' = to turn on/off the computer.
examples: Mon ordinateur ne fonctionne pas.
My computer is not working.
Elle travaille sur son ordinateur portable.
She is working on her laptop.
standard.special: The word 'ordinateur' was invented specifically for French — most other languages borrowed 'computer' directly.

standard.notes: 'Avoir une réunion' = to have a meeting. 'La réunion commence à neuf heures' = the meeting starts at nine. In French offices, 'les réunions' can be formal (boardroom) or informal ('une réunion debout' = a standing meeting). 'Annuler une réunion' = to cancel a meeting.
examples: J'ai une réunion importante à dix heures.
I have an important meeting at ten o'clock.
La réunion dure deux heures.
The meeting lasts two hours.

standard.notes: Irregular plural: 'les travaux' (works / roadworks / renovation works). 'Aller au travail' = to go to work. 'Chercher du travail' = to look for work. 'Bonne chance au travail !' = Good luck at work! 'Les travaux' on a road sign means roadworks ahead.
examples: Mon travail commence à huit heures.
My work starts at eight o'clock.
Il cherche du travail depuis un mois.
He has been looking for work for a month.
standard.special: Irregular plural: 'les travaux' (public works, roadworks, renovation).

advanced.notes: 'Bureau' has two meanings: the room ('le bureau' = the office) and the piece of furniture ('un bureau' = a desk). 'Au bureau' = at the office. 'Travailler dans un bureau' = to work in an office. 'Le bureau de poste' = the post office. Context makes the meaning clear.
examples: Je travaille au bureau tous les jours.
I work at the office every day.
Mon bureau est au troisième étage.
My office is on the third floor.

advanced.notes: Feminine form: 'la collègue'. 'Mes collègues' = my colleagues. French workplace culture values formal politeness — using 'vous' with colleagues is still common in many sectors, especially at first. 'Un bon collègue' = a good colleague / a good mate at work.
examples: Je déjeune avec mes collègues.
I have lunch with my colleagues.
Ma collègue s'appelle Sophie.
My (female) colleague is called Sophie.

advanced.notes: The French word 'ordinateur' was coined in 1955 by linguist Jacques Perret for IBM France — it comes from Latin 'ordinator' (one who orders). Informal: 'l'ordi'. 'L'ordinateur portable' = the laptop. 'Allumer / éteindre l'ordinateur' = to turn on/off the computer.
examples: Mon ordinateur ne fonctionne pas.
My computer is not working.
Elle travaille sur son ordinateur portable.
She is working on her laptop.
advanced.special: The word 'ordinateur' was invented specifically for French — most other languages borrowed 'computer' directly.

advanced.notes: 'Avoir une réunion' = to have a meeting. 'La réunion commence à neuf heures' = the meeting starts at nine. In French offices, 'les réunions' can be formal (boardroom) or informal ('une réunion debout' = a standing meeting). 'Annuler une réunion' = to cancel a meeting.
examples: J'ai une réunion importante à dix heures.
I have an important meeting at ten o'clock.
La réunion dure deux heures.
The meeting lasts two hours.

advanced.notes: Irregular plural: 'les travaux' (works / roadworks / renovation works). 'Aller au travail' = to go to work. 'Chercher du travail' = to look for work. 'Bonne chance au travail !' = Good luck at work! 'Les travaux' on a road sign means roadworks ahead.
examples: Mon travail commence à huit heures.
My work starts at eight o'clock.
Il cherche du travail depuis un mois.
He has been looking for work for a month.
advanced.special: Irregular plural: 'les travaux' (public works, roadworks, renovation).

advanced.notes: Feminine form: 'la patronne'. 'Mon patron est strict' = My boss is strict. In a café or restaurant context, 'le patron' is also the owner. Informal: 'le chef' or 'le boss'. 'Parler à son patron' = to speak to one's boss. Very common in everyday French conversation.
examples: Mon patron arrive à neuf heures.
My boss arrives at nine o'clock.
Elle est la patronne de cette entreprise.
She is the owner/boss of this company.

advanced.notes: 'Chercher un emploi' = to look for a job. 'L'emploi du temps' = the timetable / schedule. 'Le taux de chômage' = the unemployment rate. 'Emploi' is more formal than 'travail' and often used in official contexts like CVs and job ads ('offre d'emploi' = job offer/advert).
examples: Elle a trouvé un emploi dans une banque.
She found a job at a bank.
Les offres d'emploi sont en ligne.
The job adverts are online.

dialogue: B
Bienvenue dans l'équipe ! Je m'appelle Thomas. Je vais t'expliquer comment ça marche.
A
Merci ! J'ai une question — à qui est-ce que je parle pour les problèmes d'ordinateur ?
B
Tu parles à Marc — je lui envoie un message pour te présenter.
A
Et pour la réunion ce matin — qu'est-ce que je dois apporter ?
B
Je lui donne le dossier ce matin. Toi, tu leur envoies l'email avec les notes.
A
D'accord. Et le travail commence à quelle heure ici ?
B
À neuf heures. La réunion est à dix heures. Le bureau est au deuxième étage.
A
Parfait. Merci beaucoup pour ton aide !

questions.question: Who should the new employee contact for computer problems?
What does Thomas say he will give to the new employee for the meeting?

standard.summary: Thomas welcomes a new employee on their first day and explains the office setup. The new employee should speak to Marc for computer issues, and Thomas will send Marc a message to introduce them. Thomas will give the new employee the file for the morning meeting, and the new employee needs to send the team an email with notes. Work starts at nine, the meeting is at ten, and the office is on the second floor.

dialogue: B
Bienvenue, Marie ! Je suis Julien, ton collègue. Je vais tout t'expliquer.
A
Merci, Julien. À qui est-ce que je rapporte directement ?
B
Tu rapportes au patron — je lui parle ce matin pour lui dire que tu commences aujourd'hui.
A
Et pour les réunions — je leur envoie les comptes rendus ?
B
Oui. Tu leur envoies un email après chaque réunion. Je t'aide à trouver l'emploi du temps.
A
Mon ordinateur fonctionne — parfait. Et le travail se passe bien ici ?
B
Oui, l'équipe est sympa. Je lui donne souvent des idées et il les écoute.
A
C'est rassurant ! Je lui parle à la réunion de dix heures alors.

questions.question: Who does the new employee report to directly?
What does the new employee need to send after each meeting?
What does Julien say about the boss?
options: He does not reply to emails

advanced.summary: Julien welcomes Marie on her first day. She reports directly to the boss, and Julien will speak to the boss that morning to inform him of her start. After each meeting, Marie needs to send the team summary emails. Julien offers to help her find the schedule. The boss listens to ideas, which Marie finds reassuring. The first meeting is at ten o'clock.

standard.title: Indirect Object Pronouns (lui, leur)
standard.explanation:
**Indirect object pronouns** replace a noun that follows the preposition 'à' — typically a person you are giving something to, telling something to, or speaking to.

**The two forms:**
| Replaces | Pronoun | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| à + singular (him or her) | **lui** | to him / to her |
| à + plural (them) | **leur** | to them |

**Word order:** The pronoun goes **before the verb**.
- *Je parle à Marc.* → *Je lui parle.* (I speak to him.)
- *Tu envoies un email à l'équipe.* → *Tu leur envoies un email.* (You send them an email.)

**Common verbs that use indirect objects:**
- *parler à* (to speak to), *donner à* (to give to), *envoyer à* (to send to)
- *dire à* (to say to), *demander à* (to ask), *montrer à* (to show to)
- *répondre à* (to reply to), *téléphoner à* (to phone)

**Important:** 'Lui' and 'leur' never change — 'lui' = to him AND to her (no separate feminine form).

**Examples in context:**
- *Je lui donne le dossier.* = I give him/her the file.
- *Tu leur envoies l'email.* = You send them the email.
- *Il lui parle tous les matins.* = He speaks to him/her every morning.

examples.english: Do you give them the notes?

examples.french: Elle lui parle à la réunion.
examples.english: She speaks to him/her at the meeting.
He explains the project to them.

drills.question: Replace 'à Marie': 'Je parle à Marie.' → 'Je ___ parle.'
drills.explanation: 'À Marie' (singular) → 'lui'. Indirect object pronoun for singular (him/her) = lui.

drills.question: Complete: Je ___ envoie l'email. (I send them the email.)
drills.explanation: 'Leur' = to them (plural indirect object). Je leur envoie l'email.

drills.question: Which sentence means 'She gives him the file'?
drills.explanation: 'Lui' = indirect object pronoun (to him/her), placed before the verb: elle lui donne.

drills.question: Complete: Il ___ dit bonjour. (He says hello to them.)
drills.explanation: 'Dire à quelqu'un' = to say to someone. Plural → leur: il leur dit bonjour.

drills.question: What is the indirect object pronoun for 'to her'?
drills.explanation: 'Lui' = to him OR to her (singular). There is no separate feminine form for the indirect object pronoun.

advanced.title: Indirect Object Pronouns — lui, leur with All Tenses & Common Verbs
advanced.explanation:
**Indirect object pronouns** (pronoms objets indirects) replace 'à + person' constructions. They attach before the conjugated verb in most tenses.

**The pronouns:**
| Replaces | Pronoun |
|---|---|
| à lui / à elle (singular) | **lui** |
| à eux / à elles (plural) | **leur** |

**Placement rules:**

**1. Simple tenses (present, imperfect, future):** Before the conjugated verb.
- *Je lui parle.* | *Je lui parlais.* | *Je lui parlerai.*

**2. With infinitive constructions:** Before the infinitive.
- *Je vais lui donner le dossier.* = I'm going to give him/her the file.
- *Il doit leur envoyer les notes.* = He must send them the notes.

**3. Compound tenses (passé composé):** Before the auxiliary.
- *Je lui ai envoyé un message.* = I sent him/her a message.
- *Nous leur avons expliqué la situation.* = We explained the situation to them.

**4. Negative sentences:** The pronoun stays before the verb.
- *Je ne lui parle pas.* = I don't speak to him/her.
- *Il ne leur répond jamais.* = He never replies to them.

**5. With imperative (commands):**
- Affirmative: pronoun goes AFTER verb with a hyphen: *Parle-lui ! Donnez-leur le rapport !*
- Negative: pronoun stays before verb: *Ne lui parle pas ! Ne leur envoie pas ça !*

**Key verbs using indirect objects:**
- *téléphoner à, écrire à, répondre à, conseiller à, expliquer à, montrer à, offrir à, promettre à*

**Do not confuse with direct object pronouns:**
- *Je le vois.* (I see him — direct) vs *Je lui parle.* (I speak to him — indirect)

examples.french: Je vais lui donner le rapport demain.
examples.english: I'm going to give him/her the report tomorrow.

examples.french: Il leur a expliqué le projet.
examples.english: He explained the project to them.

examples.french: Ne leur envoie pas cet email.

drills.question: Which is correct? 'I sent her the notes.'
options: Je lui ai envoyé les notes.
Je ai lui envoyé les notes.
drills.correctAnswer: Je lui ai envoyé les notes.
drills.explanation: Passé composé: indirect object pronoun goes before the auxiliary 'ai'. Je lui ai envoyé.

drills.question: Complete: Donne-___ le dossier ! (Give him/her the file! — imperative)
drills.explanation: In affirmative imperatives, the pronoun follows the verb with a hyphen: donne-lui.

drills.question: How do you say 'He must send them the report'?
options: Il doit envoyer leur le rapport.
Il doit leur envoyer le rapport.
Il leur doit envoyer le rapport.
Il doit envoyer le rapport leur.
drills.correctAnswer: Il doit leur envoyer le rapport.
drills.explanation: With modal + infinitive, the indirect object pronoun goes before the infinitive: leur envoyer.

drills.question: Complete: Ne ___ parle pas de ça ! (Don't talk to them about that!)
drills.explanation: In negative imperatives, the pronoun stays before the verb: ne leur parle pas.

drills.question: What is the difference between 'Je le vois' and 'Je lui parle'?
drills.explanation: 'Je le vois' = I see him (direct object — no preposition). 'Je lui parle' = I speak to him (indirect — replaces 'à lui').

drills.question: Complete: Je ne ___ réponds jamais. (I never reply to her.)
drills.explanation: 'Répondre à quelqu'un' → indirect object. Singular → lui. Je ne lui réponds jamais.

drills.question: Which sentence correctly says 'We explained the situation to them'?
options: Nous avons leur expliqué la situation.
Nous leur avons expliqué la situation.
Nous avons expliqué leur la situation.
Nous leur expliqué avons la situation.
drills.correctAnswer: Nous leur avons expliqué la situation.
drills.explanation: In passé composé, the indirect object pronoun goes before the auxiliary: leur avons expliqué.

drills.question: Complete: Je vais ___ téléphoner ce soir. (I'm going to phone him tonight.)
drills.explanation: With 'aller + infinitive', the indirect object pronoun goes before the infinitive: vais lui téléphoner.

standard.question: What does 'la réunion' mean?
standard.explanation: 'La réunion' = the meeting. 'Avoir une réunion' = to have a meeting.

standard.question: Which sentence means 'I send her an email'?
standard.explanation: 'Lui' = indirect object pronoun for him/her (singular). Je lui envoie = I send to him/her.

standard.question: Complete: Je ___ explique le projet. (I explain the project to them.)
standard.explanation: 'Leur' = indirect object pronoun for them (plural). Je leur explique.

standard.question: What is the irregular plural of 'le travail'?
standard.explanation: 'Le travail' has the irregular plural 'les travaux' (works/roadworks). A very common word.

standard.question: What is special about the French word 'ordinateur'?
options: It was invented specifically for French by a linguist
standard.correctAnswer: It was invented specifically for French by a linguist
standard.explanation: 'Ordinateur' was coined in 1955 by linguist Jacques Perret for IBM France — most languages borrowed 'computer' directly.

advanced.question: How do you say 'Don't tell them about it'?
advanced.explanation: In negative imperatives, the pronoun goes before the verb: ne leur dis pas ça.

advanced.question: Complete: Elle ___ a donné l'emploi du temps. (She gave him/her the timetable.)
advanced.explanation: Passé composé: indirect object pronoun before the auxiliary. Elle lui a donné.

advanced.question: How do you say 'He is going to phone them'?
advanced.explanation: With aller + infinitive, the indirect object pronoun goes before the infinitive: va leur téléphoner.

advanced.question: What does 'le patron / la patronne' mean?
advanced.explanation: 'Le patron / la patronne' = the boss or owner. Common in everyday French for both workplace and café/restaurant owners.

advanced.question: Complete: Parle-___ de la réunion. (Tell him/her about the meeting. — imperative)
advanced.explanation: In affirmative imperatives, the indirect object pronoun follows the verb with a hyphen: parle-lui.

advanced.question: Which is an 'offre d'emploi'?
advanced.explanation: 'Une offre d'emploi' = a job offer or job advertisement. 'Emploi' is the formal word for job/employment.

standard.scenario: Describe your typical workday to a new colleague. Explain who you report to, what tasks you do, and mention the meeting. Use indirect object pronouns (lui, leur) to describe communication.
keyPhrases: Je travaille au bureau de… (I work at the … office.)
Je lui envoie… / Je leur donne… (I send him/her… / I give them…)
J'ai une réunion à… heures. (I have a meeting at… o'clock.)
Je parle à mon collègue / mon patron. (I speak to my colleague / my boss.)
Mon travail commence à… (My work starts at…)
standard.modelAnswer: Mon travail commence à neuf heures. Le matin, j'allume mon ordinateur et je lui envoie un email — à mon patron. À dix heures, j'ai une réunion avec mes collègues. Je leur explique le projet de la semaine. L'après-midi, je travaille au bureau et je leur donne les documents.
standard.modelAnswerExplanation: The answer gives a time-ordered description, uses 'lui' for the singular boss and 'leur' for the plural team, names key office vocab (ordinateur, réunion, bureau), and naturally integrates indirect object pronouns into realistic sentences.
standard.tip: Remember: 'lui' = to him OR to her (singular). 'Leur' = to them (plural). Both go before the verb!

advanced.scenario: It is your first day at a new job. Explain your role to a colleague: who you report to, what you send/give to whom, and how you use indirect object pronouns. Use passé composé and imperative forms naturally.
keyPhrases: Je rapporte directement au patron. (I report directly to the boss.)
Je lui ai envoyé… / Je leur ai donné… (I sent him/her… / I gave them…)
Il doit leur envoyer le rapport. (He must send them the report.)
Ne leur parle pas encore de ça. (Don't talk to them about that yet.)
advanced.modelAnswer: Bonjour ! Je suis nouveau ici — je rapporte directement au patron. Ce matin, je lui ai envoyé un email pour me présenter. Pour les réunions, je dois leur envoyer un compte rendu après chaque réunion. Mon collègue m'a dit : 'Parle-lui de tes idées — il les écoute !' Je ne leur ai pas encore parlé du nouveau projet. Je vais leur expliquer ça à la réunion de dix heures.
advanced.modelAnswerExplanation: The answer uses lui (singular: boss) and leur (plural: team) across multiple tenses — passé composé (lui ai envoyé), modal + infinitive (leur envoyer), affirmative imperative (parle-lui), negative passé composé (ne leur ai pas encore parlé), and aller + infinitive (leur expliquer).
